articles.title: A grad student’s wild idea sparks a major aging breakthrough
articles.description: A casual conversation between graduate students helped spark a breakthrough in aging research at Mayo Clinic. Researchers discovered that tiny synthetic DNA molecules called aptamers can selectively attach to senescent “zombie cells,” which are linked to aging, cancer, and neurodegenerative disease. The method could eventually help scientists identify and target these cells in living tissue with far greater precision.

articles.title: A 47-year study reveals when strength and fitness start to fade
articles.description: A groundbreaking Swedish study that tracked people for nearly 50 years has revealed when the body’s physical decline quietly begins. Researchers found that fitness, strength, and muscle endurance start slipping around age 35, with the decline accelerating over time. But there’s an encouraging twist: adults who became active later in life still improved their physical performance by up to 10 percent.

articles.title: Scientists “bottle the sun” with a liquid battery that stores solar energy
articles.description: Scientists at UC Santa Barbara have created a remarkable new material that works like a “rechargeable solar battery,” storing sunlight inside tiny molecules and releasing it later as heat — even long after the sun goes down. Inspired by reversible changes found in DNA and photochromic sunglasses, the system captures solar energy without relying on bulky batteries or the electrical grid. The molecule can hold energy for years and packs more energy per kilogram than lithium-ion batteries.

Visit comes after US-Cuba relations deteriorated significantly, and as the island nation declared it had ‘absolutely no fuel’ because of US blockade

The meeting took place “in a context marked by the complexity of bilateral relations, with the aim of contributing to the political dialogue between both nations”, a statement said.

articles.title: Honda’s hybrid future starts with new Accord and RDX prototypes
articles.description:
Honda revealed prototypes of two new hybrid models, an Accord sedan and the Acura RDX SUV, during its annual business briefing this week, built on a platform that it says will begin launching next year. The RDX was announced earlier this year as Honda's first SUV to feature the next-gen version of its two-motor hybrid system. 
Honda also announced it's dropping the goal of having one-fifth of its sales be EVs by 2030, and for 100 percent of its sales to come from EVs and fuel cell vehicles by 2040. Honda now plans to "reallocate more development and production resources into hybrid models."
Honda is planning to launch 15 next-generation h …

articles.title: Supreme Court allows abortion pill to remain available by mail

articles.title: Closing time
articles.description:
Today was closing arguments in the Musk v. Altman trial, and I almost feel bad writing about the unbelievable demolition derby I just witnessed. Steven Molo, Musk's lawyer, stumbled over his words. He at one point called Greg Brockman - a co-defendant - Greg Altman. He erroneously claimed that Musk wasn't asking for money and had to be corrected by the judge. He made it clear we've heard from many liars over the past few weeks, but offered little evidence for Musk's actual legal claims.
OpenAI's lawyer, Sarah Eddy, countered this by simply arranging the mountain of evidence that the company introduced in chronological order. She didn't spen …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Stocks, Bonds Decline on Concerns About Inflation: Markets Wrap
articles.description: Stocks fell with bonds as rising oil prices deepened concern about inflation and as investors questioned the longevity of the artificial intelligence trade.

articles.title: Supreme Court preserves access to widely used abortion pill, while lawsuit plays out
articles.description: The Supreme Court on Thursday preserved women’s access to the abortion drug mifepristone, rejecting lower-court restrictions while a lawsuit continues.

articles.title: Supreme Court, over two dissents, upholds abortion pills sent by mail, for now
articles.description: The court rejects an antiabortion challenge to mifepristone, an FDA-approved medication for ending early pregnancies.

articles.title: The original Doom soundtrack is officially in the Library of Congress
articles.description: Other new inductees include Beyonce's 'Single Ladies' and Taylor Swift's '1989.'

articles.description: Hopelessly one-sided Indo-U.S. trade deal that was really a steal by the U.S., says Congress leader Jairam Ramesh in a post on X.

articles.title: How Tagore’s songs were ahead of their time in form and feeling
articles.description: Rabindrasangeet, a unique genre of Indian music, bridges classical rigour with folk spontaneity and Western influences, while anticipating modern concerns.

articles.title: Former Kerala CM Pinarayi Vijayan slams Centre for hiking fuel prices
articles.description: He said the Centre had used the rise of crude oil prices owing to the crisis in West Asia to fleece ordinary people, who would feel the bite of the fuel price hike acutely

articles.title: After NEET paper leak, Karnataka CM seeks return of state-controlled medical entrance tests
articles.description: Siddaramaiah said the Karnataka government was insisting that powers to conduct such entrance examinations should be restored to states.

articles.title: Kaynes Tech shares fall another 5% a day after crashing 20%. What brokerages fear the most?
articles.description: Kaynes Tech shares extended losses, falling another 5% after a 20% crash following weak Q4 earnings. Profit declined 22% YoY despite 26% revenue growth, which missed internal targets. Brokerages including JP Morgan, Nuvama and CLSA cut targets, citing execution concerns, margin pressure, and weak near-term visibility.

articles.title: Will lead to further inflation, growth estimates will be lowered considerably: Congress on fuel hike
Rahul Gandhi links Adani relief in US report to India FTA, targets PM Modi: ‘Not trade deal, but a bargain’
articles.description: Invoking his earlier “compromised PM” jibe, Rahul Gandhi said, “PM did not strike a trade deal, but a bargain for Adani's release.”

articles.title: Rahul Gandhi's fresh swipe at PM Modi over India-US trade deal: 'Compromised PM'
articles.description: Rahul Gandhi has accused Prime Minister Narendra Modi of striking a "deal for Adani's release" with the US, rather than a genuine trade agreement. The Congress party echoed these sentiments, labeling the pact "one-sided" and alleging it aims to close fraud cases against Gautam Adani in America, suggesting Modi is compromised and subservient to US demands.

topStories.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent did not say when these talks would happen. There are fears in the United States and China about the threats from A.I., but neither side is willing to slow down its development.

topStories.title: Trump praises Xi, says ‘wonderful things’ done during China summit
topStories.description: President Trump thanked President Xi during remarks on the second day of the U.S.-China summit. He said a lot of problems were settled during talks and indicated that the two leaders share similar feelings about wanting the war with Iran to end.

topStories.description: President Donald Trump says Chinese President Xi Jinping pledged not to supply Iran with military equipment, calling it a major win from the bilateral summit.

dailyNews.title: WATCH:  How a new drug could lengthen lives of pancreatic cancer patients
dailyNews.description: ABC News’ Linsey Davis explains the numbers behind a new pancreatic cancer treatment that almost doubles the prognosis for patients, and speaks to Dr. Brandon Huffman, whose team is behind the drug.

dailyNews.title: Cedric Alexander becomes new TNA X Division champion, crushing Leon Slater's history-making attempt
dailyNews.description: Cedric Alexander ends Leon Slater's historic X Division Championship reign, and Fabian Aichner emerges as a new challenger on TNA "Impact" Thursday.

dailyNews.title: Trump and Kennedy seek to relax safeguards for AI health care tools
dailyNews.description: Paul Boyer, a psychotherapist for Kaiser Permanente in Oakland, California, is experiencing the AI revolution firsthand. He's a little underwhelmed. The health giant has rolled out a new suite of note-taking software, made by health care AI pioneer Abridge, intended to summarize a patient's visit at supersonic speed. For many clinicians, the technology soothes one of the persistent headaches of their lives—administration and paperwork.

dailyNews.title: Why are so many U.S. CEOs in China with Trump, and what do they want?
dailyNews.description: More than a dozen American CEOs are accompanying President Trump on his trip to China. That's not unusual.

dailyNews.description: Washington and Beijing expected to extend trade truce, but analysts see only modest breakthrough in strained ties.

dailyNews.title: ICE releases wife of U.S. Army soldier after month-long detention
dailyNews.description: ICE has released the wife of an active-duty U.S. soldier after a month in detention, her husband told CBS News.

dailyNews.title: Paris Jackson wins legal battle over Michael Jackson Estate payments worth $625,000
dailyNews.description: Paris Jackson has secured a major legal victory in her dispute with the executors of Michael Jackson’s estate after a Los Angeles judge ruled that more than $625,000 in bonuses paid to outside law firms must be returned.

dailyNews.description: Oil prices rise Friday on news that China has agreed to purchase oil from America

dailyNews.description: Al Jazeera's Manuel Rapalo reports from Washington, where the first of two days of Lebanon-Israel talks concluded.

dailyNews.title: Paul Finebaum roasts Lane Kiffin for recent comments: "Trying to wipe Ole Miss off the map"
dailyNews.description: Paul Finebaum says LSU coach Lane Kiffin's racial diversity criticism of Ole Miss was motivated by bitterness over missing out on the College Football Playoff.

dailyNews.title: Inside the Congolese Hotel Where Trump Deported 15 U.S. Migrants
dailyNews.description: They were shackled and sent to Kinshasa by the Trump administration. Now they face a dangerous choice: Go back to Latin America or stay in Africa.

dailyNews.title: Why Trump’s Reflecting Pool Repairs Are in Trouble
dailyNews.description: Federal records show that the no-bid contract to repair the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool and paint it blue now costs $13.1 million. That’s about seven times the amount President Trump initially said it would cost.
